items.headline: Dow Jones Stocks: Honeywell Aerospace Looms As GE Stock Tries To Break Out
items.summary: Dow Jones stock Honeywell announced the formal board approval of plans to spin out its Honeywell Aerospace unit, as the conglomerate prepares to break up.  Honeywell stock popped above a key level.  On June 29, Honeywell's automation and aerospace businesses will become two independent, publicly traded companies.

items.headline: GE Vernova GridOS Launch Spurs Questions On Growth And Valuation
items.summary: GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) has launched GridOS for Transmission, a unified software platform for grid operators. The new solution is designed to help utilities make coordinated, near real-time decisions across the transmission grid. GridOS for Transmission targets rising electricity demand, renewable integration, and growing grid complexity. items.headline: Wolfspeed Targets High Voltage Growth With Gen 5 SiC And GE Alliance
items.summary: Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) introduced its fifth-generation silicon carbide MOSFET technology, targeting higher efficiency and performance in automotive and industrial power systems. The company also announced an alliance with GE Aerospace to speed up adoption and standardization of high-voltage silicon carbide across advanced industrial and aerospace markets.
